commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `coe-root-cause` skill row to the public corpus fixture and increments the public-corpus manifest and validation-report counts.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a skill-content PR rather than a bug report. Source and diff inspection confirm current main does not contain the `coe-root-cause` row and the PR adds it as a public-corpus fixture.

target.title: Add COE root-cause skill
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `coe-root-cause` to the ClawHub public corpus as an installable Correction of Error root-cause analysis skill.

The skill is based on Amazon's rigorous Correction of Error (COE) practice: a blameless, evidence-backed post-incident loop that focuses on root cause, corrective actions, owner accountability, and durable prevention rather than only fixing the immediate bug.

## Notes

- Adds one public corpus skill row for `coe-root-cause`.
- Keeps the skill tool-agnostic and public-safe.
- Explicitly asks agents to redact secrets, tokens, PII, customer data, and private workspace details.
- Clarifies that report-only requests should propose corrective actions unless the user asks to apply them.
- Adapts the Amazon/AWS COE pattern for agent workflow failures, false success, data loss, missed work, and brittle automation.
- Tightens the COE workflow around DRY/MECE principles: each report fact has one home, and failure classification is split into exactly one primary failure surface plus evidence-backed cause tags.
- Supersedes the misplaced OpenClaw core draft PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88484

## Sources

- AWS Cloud Operations Blog: [Why you should develop a Correction of Error (COE)](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/mt/why-you-should-develop-a-correction-of-error-coe/)
- AWS Well-Architected Framework: [REL12-BP02 Perform post-incident analysis](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/framework/rel_testing_resiliency_rca_resiliency.html)

## Verification

- `bun run validate:public-corpus`
- `bun test scripts/public-corpus/validate.test.ts scripts/public-corpus/seed-public-corpus.test.ts`
- `bun run ci:static`
- DRY/MECE smoke check over the ClawHub public-corpus row and matching GStack skill.
- Privacy scan on the added diff for local/private identifiers and token patterns.
- Local AI review: a separate private Codex review session inspected the branch diff before this PR was opened. It did not post to GitHub, tag external review bots, or expose repo contents outside the local workflow. The review checked fixture shape, public/private leakage, ClawHub fit, source/provenance wording, and missing validation steps. It initially flagged stale fixture counts and an uncommitted diff during preparation; both were fixed before push.
